120	
121	        n_train = int(len(self.train_dataset) * data_cfg["train_split"])
122	        n_val = int(len(self.train_dataset) * data_cfg["val_split"])
123	        n_test = len(self.train_dataset) - n_train - n_val
124	        self.train_set, self.val_set, self.test_set = random_split(
125	            self.train_dataset,
126	            [n_train, n_val, n_test],
127	            generator=torch.Generator().manual_seed(42),
128	        )
129	
130	        # Pin memory for async GPU transfer
131	        pin_memory = self.device.type == "cuda"
132	        num_workers = data_cfg.get("num_workers", 0)
133	
134	        self.train_loader = DataLoader(
135	            self.train_set,
136	            batch_size=data_cfg["batch_size"],
137	            shuffle=True,
138	            num_workers=num_workers,
139	            collate_fn=collate_fn,
140	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
141	            drop_last=True,
142	            prefetch_factor=2 if num_workers > 0 else None,
143	        )
144	        self.val_loader = DataLoader(
145	            self.val_set,
146	            batch_size=data_cfg["batch_size"],
147	            shuffle=False,
148	            num_workers=num_workers,
149	            collate_fn=collate_fn,
150	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
151	            prefetch_factor=2 if num_workers > 0 else None,
152	        )
153	
154	        # Model
155	        enc_cfg = model_cfg.get("encoder", {})
156	        self.model = StreamingTCNModel(
157	            n_mels=data_cfg.get("n_mels", 128),
158	            embedding_dim=enc_cfg.get("embedding_dim", 128),
159	            num_bands=self.num_bands,
160	            channels=enc_cfg.get("channels", 128),
161	            num_blocks=enc_cfg.get("num_blocks", 4),
162	            num_stacks=enc_cfg.get("num_stacks", 2),
163	            sample_rate=self.sample_rate,
164	            n_fft=self.n_fft,
165	        ).to(self.device)
166	
167	        # STFT frontend
168	        self.frontend = STFTFrontend(
169	            n_fft=self.n_fft,
170	            hop_length=data_cfg.get("hop_length", 256),
171	            win_length=self.n_fft,
172	            mel_bins=data_cfg.get("n_mels", 128),
173	            sample_rate=self.sample_rate,
174	        ).to(self.device)
175	
176	        # Loss
177	        self.criterion = MultiTypeEQLoss(
178	            n_fft=self.n_fft,
179	            sample_rate=self.sample_rate,
180	            lambda_param=loss_cfg.get("lambda_param", 0.0),
181	            lambda_gain=loss_cfg.get("lambda_gain", 3.0),
182	            lambda_freq=loss_cfg.get("lambda_freq", 1.0),
183	            lambda_q=loss_cfg.get("lambda_q", 0.5),
184	            lambda_type=loss_cfg.get("lambda_type", 0.5),
185	            lambda_spectral=loss_cfg.get("lambda_spectral", 1.0),
186	            lambda_hmag=loss_cfg.get("lambda_hmag", 0.3),
187	            lambda_activity=loss_cfg.get("lambda_activity", 0.1),
188	            lambda_spread=loss_cfg.get("lambda_spread", 0.05),
189	            lambda_embed_var=loss_cfg.get("lambda_embed_var", 0.3),
190	            lambda_contrastive=loss_cfg.get("lambda_contrastive", 0.05),
191	            embed_var_threshold=loss_cfg.get("embed_var_threshold", 0.1),
192	        )
193	
194	        # Hungarian matcher for fair validation metrics (matches target ordering to predictions)
195	        self.matcher = HungarianBandMatcher(lambda_freq=1.0, lambda_q=0.5)
196	
197	        # Optimizer: 8-bit or standard
198	        if self.use_8bit_optimizer:
199	            self.optimizer = bnb.optim.AdamW8bit(
200	                self.model.parameters(),
201	                lr=model_cfg["learning_rate"],
202	                weight_decay=model_cfg["weight_decay"],
203	            )
204	        else:
205	            self.optimizer = torch.optim.AdamW(
206	                self.model.parameters(),
207	                lr=model_cfg["learning_rate"],
208	                weight_decay=model_cfg["weight_decay"],
209	            )
210	        self.scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(
211	            self.optimizer, T_max=self.max_epochs, eta_min=1e-6
212	        )
213	
214	        # LR warmup: linear warmup over first 500 steps, then hand off to CosineAnnealing
215	        warmup_steps = 500
216	        warmup_scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.LinearLR(
217	            self.optimizer, start_factor=1e-3, end_factor=1.0, total_iters=warmup_steps
218	        )
219	        self.scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.SequentialLR(
220	            self.optimizer,
221	            schedulers=[warmup_scheduler, self.scheduler],
222	            milestones=[warmup_steps],
223	        )
224	
225	        self.gradient_accumulation_steps = trainer_cfg.get(
226	            "gradient_accumulation_steps", 1
227	        )
228	        self.global_step = 0
229	        self.best_val_loss = float("inf")
230	        self.history = []
231	
232	        # bf16-mixed precision: autocast context (no GradScaler needed for bf16)
233	        self.use_bf16 = trainer_cfg.get("precision", "").lower() == "bf16-mixed"
234	        if self.use_bf16:
235	            device_type = "cuda" if self.device.type == "cuda" else "cpu"
236	            self.autocast_ctx = torch.autocast(device_type, dtype=torch.bfloat16)
237	        else:
238	            self.autocast_ctx = nullcontext()
239	
240	        # Curriculum stages from config
241	        self.curriculum_stages = self.cfg.get("curriculum", {}).get("stages", [])
242	        self._current_stage_idx = -1
243	
244	        # DeepSpeed initialization
245	        if self.use_deepspeed:
246	            ds_config_path = trainer_cfg.get(
247	                "deepspeed_config", "conf/deepspeed_config.json"
248	            )
249	            if os.path.exists(ds_config_path):
250	                with open(ds_config_path, "r") as f:
251	                    ds_config = json.load(f)
252	                self.model, self.optimizer, _, _ = deepspeed.initialize(
253	                    model=self.model,
254	                    optimizer=self.optimizer,
255	                    config=ds_config,
256	                )
257	                print(f"  [opt] DeepSpeed initialized with config: {ds_config_path}")
258	            else:
259	                print(
260	                    f"  [opt] WARNING: DeepSpeed config not found at {ds_config_path}, disabling"
261	                )
262	                self.use_deepspeed = False
263	
264	        # torch.compile after DeepSpeed init to avoid conflicts
265	        if self.use_torch_compile:
266	            self.model = torch.compile(self.model)
267	            print("  [opt] torch.compile applied to model")
268	
269	    def _prepare_input(self, batch):
270	        """Get mel-spectrogram from precomputed cache or compute on-the-fly."""
271	        if "wet_mel" in batch:
272	            # Use precomputed mel-spectrogram
273	            return batch["wet_mel"].to(self.device, non_blocking=True)
274	        # Fallback: compute from audio
275	        wet_audio = batch["wet_audio"].to(self.device, non_blocking=True)
276	        mel_spec = self.frontend.mel_spectrogram(wet_audio)
277	        return mel_spec.squeeze(1)
278	
279	    def train_one_epoch(self, epoch):
280	        self.model.train()
281	        epoch_loss = 0.0
282	        n_batches = 0
283	        n_nan_batches = 0
284	
285	        # Accumulate per-component losses for epoch-level logging
286	        component_accum = {}
287	
288	        self.optimizer.zero_grad(set_to_none=True)
289	
290	        for batch_idx, batch in enumerate(self.train_loader):
291	            mel_frames = self._prepare_input(batch)
292	            target_gain = batch["gain"].to(self.device, non_blocking=True)
293	            target_freq = batch["freq"].to(self.device, non_blocking=True)
294	            target_q = batch["q"].to(self.device, non_blocking=True)
295	            target_ft = batch["filter_type"].to(self.device, non_blocking=True)
296	
297	            # Forward (wrapped in autocast for bf16-mixed precision)
298	            with self.autocast_ctx:
299	                output = self.model(mel_frames)
300	                pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q = output["params"]
301	
302	                # Ground truth frequency response
303	                target_H_mag = self.model.dsp_cascade(
304	                    target_gain,
305	                    target_freq,
306	                    target_q,
307	                    n_fft=self.n_fft,
308	                    filter_type=target_ft,
309	                )
310	
311	                # Loss (pass embedding for anti-collapse regularization)
312	                total_loss, components = self.criterion(
313	                    pred_gain,
314	                    pred_freq,
315	                    pred_q,
316	                    output["type_logits"],
317	                    output["H_mag"],
318	                    target_gain,
319	                    target_freq,
320	                    target_q,
321	                    target_ft,
322	                    target_H_mag,
323	                    embedding=output["embedding"],
324	                )
325	
326	            # Skip batch if loss is NaN (from early training instability)
327	            if not torch.isfinite(total_loss):
328	                self.optimizer.zero_grad(set_to_none=True)
329	                n_nan_batches += 1
330	                if n_nan_batches >= 10:
331	                    print(
332	                        f"  [train] WARNING: {n_nan_batches} NaN batches in epoch {epoch}, stopping epoch early"
333	                    )
334	                    break
335	                continue
336	
337	            # Scale loss for gradient accumulation
338	            scaled_loss = total_loss / self.gradient_accumulation_steps
339	
340	            if self.use_deepspeed:
341	                self.model.backward(scaled_loss)
342	            else:
343	                scaled_loss.backward()
344	
345	            if (batch_idx + 1) % self.gradient_accumulation_steps == 0:
346	                if not self.use_deepspeed:
347	                    grad_norm = torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(
348	                        self.model.parameters(), max_norm=1.0
349	                    )
350	                    if not torch.isfinite(grad_norm):
351	                        self.optimizer.zero_grad(set_to_none=True)
352	                        n_nan_batches += 1
353	                        continue
354	
355	                    # Per-parameter gradient norm monitoring (logged every N steps)
356	                    if self.global_step % self.log_every == 0:
357	                        grad_parts = []
358	                        for name, param in self.model.named_parameters():
359	                            if param.grad is not None and param.grad.norm() > 0:
360	                                if "gain" in name:
361	                                    grad_parts.append(
362	                                        ("gain", param.grad.norm().item())
363	                                    )
364	                                elif "freq" in name or "log_freq" in name:
365	                                    grad_parts.append(
366	                                        ("freq", param.grad.norm().item())
367	                                    )
368	                                elif "q_head" in name:
369	                                    grad_parts.append(("q", param.grad.norm().item()))
370	                                elif "classif" in name or "type" in name:
371	                                    grad_parts.append(
372	                                        ("type", param.grad.norm().item())
373	                                    )
374	                        if grad_parts:
375	                            grouped = {}
376	                            for k, v in grad_parts:
377	                                grouped.setdefault(k, []).append(v)
378	                            avg_grads = {k: sum(v) / len(v) for k, v in grouped.items()}
379	                            grad_str = " | ".join(
380	                                f"grad_{k}={v:.4f}"
381	                                for k, v in sorted(avg_grads.items())
382	                            )
383	                            print(f"  [grads] step={self.global_step} {grad_str}")
384	
385	                    self.optimizer.step()
386	                else:
387	                    self.model.step()
388	
389	                self.optimizer.zero_grad(set_to_none=True)
390	                self.global_step += 1
391	
392	            epoch_loss += total_loss.item()
393	            n_batches += 1
394	
395	            # Accumulate per-component losses
396	            for k, v in components.items():
397	                val = v.item() if isinstance(v, torch.Tensor) else v
398	                component_accum[k] = component_accum.get(k, 0.0) + val
399	
400	            if self.global_step % self.log_every == 0:
401	                lr = self.optimizer.param_groups[0]["lr"]
402	                parts = [
403	                    f"step={self.global_step} loss={total_loss.item():.4f} lr={lr:.2e}"
404	                ]
405	                for k, v in components.items():
406	                    parts.append(f"{k}={v:.4f}")
407	                # Log embedding variance diagnostic (monitors anti-collapse health)
408	                if "embed_var_loss" in components:
409	                    embed_var = output["embedding"].var(dim=0).mean().item()
410	                    parts.append(f"embed_var={embed_var:.4f}")
411	                print(f"  [train] " + " | ".join(parts))
412	
413	        if n_nan_batches > 0:
414	            print(f"  [train] Epoch {epoch}: {n_nan_batches} NaN batches skipped")
415	
416	        # Per-component epoch averages
417	        if n_batches > 0:
418	            comp_strs = []
419	            for k in sorted(component_accum.keys()):
420	                avg = component_accum[k] / n_batches
421	                comp_strs.append(f"{k}={avg:.4f}")
422	            print(f"  [train] Epoch {epoch} components: " + " | ".join(comp_strs))
423	
424	        return epoch_loss / max(n_batches, 1)
425	
426	    @torch.no_grad()
427	    def validate(self, epoch):
428	        self.model.eval()
429	        val_loss = 0.0
430	        n_batches = 0
431	        param_maes = {"gain": [], "gain_raw": [], "freq": [], "q": [], "type_acc": []}
432	
433	        for batch in self.val_loader:
434	            mel_frames = self._prepare_input(batch)
435	            target_gain = batch["gain"].to(self.device, non_blocking=True)
436	            target_freq = batch["freq"].to(self.device, non_blocking=True)
437	            target_q = batch["q"].to(self.device, non_blocking=True)
438	            target_ft = batch["filter_type"].to(self.device, non_blocking=True)
439	
440	            with self.autocast_ctx:
441	                output = self.model(mel_frames)
442	                pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q = output["params"]
443	
444	                target_H_mag = self.model.dsp_cascade(
445	                    target_gain,
446	                    target_freq,
447	                    target_q,
448	                    n_fft=self.n_fft,
449	                    filter_type=target_ft,
450	                )
451	
452	                total_loss, _ = self.criterion(
453	                    pred_gain,
454	                    pred_freq,
455	                    pred_q,
456	                    output["type_logits"],
457	                    output["H_mag"],
458	                    target_gain,
459	                    target_freq,
460	                    target_q,
461	                    target_ft,
462	                    target_H_mag,
463	                    embedding=output["embedding"],
464	                )
465	
466	            val_loss += total_loss.item()
467	            n_batches += 1
468	
469	            # Hungarian matching for fair MAE computation
470	            matched_gain, matched_freq, matched_q = self.matcher(
471	                pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q, target_gain, target_freq, target_q
472	            )
473	
474	            # Metrics (matched = fair, raw = for comparison)
475	            param_maes["gain"].append((pred_gain - matched_gain).abs().mean().item())
476	            param_maes["gain_raw"].append((pred_gain - target_gain).abs().mean().item())
477	            param_maes["freq"].append(
478	                (torch.log2(pred_freq / (matched_freq + 1e-8))).abs().mean().item()
479	            )
480	            param_maes["q"].append(
481	                (torch.log10(pred_q / (matched_q + 1e-8))).abs().mean().item()
482	            )
483	            param_maes["type_acc"].append(
484	                (output["filter_type"] == target_ft).float().mean().item()
485	            )
486	
487	        avg_val_loss = val_loss / max(n_batches, 1)
488	        metrics = {k: sum(v) / len(v) for k, v in param_maes.items() if v}
489	
490	        print(
491	            f"  [val] epoch={epoch} val_loss={avg_val_loss:.4f} "
492	            f"gain_mae={metrics.get('gain', 0):.2f}dB "
493	            f"gain_raw={metrics.get('gain_raw', 0):.2f}dB "
494	            f"freq_mae={metrics.get('freq', 0):.3f}oct "
495	            f"q_mae={metrics.get('q', 0):.3f}dec "
496	            f"type_acc={metrics.get('type_acc', 0):.1%}"
497	        )
498	        return avg_val_loss, metrics
499	
500	    def save_checkpoint(self, epoch, val_loss, is_best=False):
501	        ckpt_dir = Path("checkpoints")
502	        ckpt_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
503	
504	        if self.use_deepspeed:
505	            # DeepSpeed handles checkpointing
506	            ckpt_path = ckpt_dir / f"epoch_{epoch:03d}"
507	            self.model.save_checkpoint(str(ckpt_path), tag=f"epoch_{epoch}")
508	            if is_best:
509	                self.model.save_checkpoint(str(ckpt_dir / "best"), tag="best")
510	        else:
511	            state = {
512	                "epoch": epoch,
513	                "model_state_dict": self.model.state_dict(),
514	                "optimizer_state_dict": self.optimizer.state_dict(),
515	                "val_loss": val_loss,
516	                "global_step": self.global_step,
517	            }
518	            path = ckpt_dir / f"epoch_{epoch:03d}.pt"
519	            torch.save(state, path)
520	            if is_best:
521	                best_path = ckpt_dir / "best.pt"
522	                torch.save(state, best_path)
523	            print(f"  Saved checkpoint: {path}" + (" (best)" if is_best else ""))
524	
525	    def _has_nan_weights(self):
526	        """Check if any model parameter contains NaN."""
527	        for p in self.model.parameters():
528	            if not torch.isfinite(p).all():
529	                return True
530	        return False
531	
532	    def _apply_curriculum_stage(self, epoch):
533	        """Determine and apply curriculum stage based on epoch number."""
534	        if not self.curriculum_stages:
535	            return
536	
537	        # Determine stage from cumulative epoch boundaries
538	        cumulative = 0
539	        stage_idx = 0
540	        stage_start_epoch = 0
541	        for i, stage in enumerate(self.curriculum_stages):
542	            prev_cumulative = cumulative
543	            cumulative += stage["epochs"]
544	            if epoch <= cumulative:
545	                stage_idx = i
546	                stage_start_epoch = prev_cumulative + 1
547	                break
548	        else:
549	            stage_idx = len(self.curriculum_stages) - 1
550	
551	        # Skip if stage hasn't changed (but still do intra-stage annealing)
552	        stage = self.curriculum_stages[stage_idx]
553	
554	        # Intra-stage Gumbel temperature annealing
555	        target_tau = stage.get("gumbel_temperature", 1.0)
556	        stage_epochs = stage["epochs"]
557	        epoch_in_stage = epoch - stage_start_epoch
558	        if stage_idx > 0:
559	            prev_tau = self.curriculum_stages[stage_idx - 1].get(
560	                "gumbel_temperature", 1.0
561	            )
562	        else:
563	            prev_tau = 1.0
564	
565	        # Smooth linear annealing from prev_tau to target_tau within stage
566	        progress = epoch_in_stage / max(stage_epochs - 1, 1)
567	        current_tau = prev_tau + (target_tau - prev_tau) * progress
568	        current_tau = max(current_tau, 0.05)  # floor
569	        self.model.param_head.gumbel_temperature.fill_(current_tau)
570	
571	        # Only re-precompute data when stage changes
572	        if stage_idx == self._current_stage_idx:
573	            return
574	
575	        self._current_stage_idx = stage_idx
576	
577	        # Restrict active filter types for the dataset sampler
578	        active_types = stage.get("filter_types", None)
579	        if active_types is not None:
580	            all_types = ["peaking", "lowshelf", "highshelf", "highpass", "lowpass"]
581	            weights = [0.0] * len(all_types)
582	            for t in active_types:
583	                if t in all_types:
584	                    weights[all_types.index(t)] = 1.0 / len(active_types)
585	            self.train_dataset.type_weights = weights
586	
587	        # Apply param ranges if specified
588	        param_ranges = stage.get("param_ranges", {})
589	        if "gain" in param_ranges:
590	            self.train_dataset.gain_range = tuple(param_ranges["gain"])
591	        if "q" in param_ranges:
592	            self.train_dataset.q_range = tuple(param_ranges["q"])
593	
594	        # Re-precompute dataset with new type_weights/param_ranges.
595	        # Without this, the cached samples from the previous stage are still used.
596	        self.train_dataset.precompute()
597	
598	        # Re-split dataset since underlying data changed
599	        n_train = int(len(self.train_dataset) * self.cfg["data"]["train_split"])
600	        n_val = int(len(self.train_dataset) * self.cfg["data"]["val_split"])
601	        n_test = len(self.train_dataset) - n_train - n_val
602	        self.train_set, self.val_set, self.test_set = random_split(
603	            self.train_dataset,
604	            [n_train, n_val, n_test],
605	            generator=torch.Generator().manual_seed(42),
606	        )
607	
608	        pin_memory = self.device.type == "cuda"
609	        num_workers = self.cfg["data"].get("num_workers", 0)
610	        self.train_loader = DataLoader(
611	            self.train_set,
612	            batch_size=self.cfg["data"]["batch_size"],
613	            shuffle=True,
614	            num_workers=num_workers,
615	            collate_fn=collate_fn,
616	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
617	            drop_last=True,
618	            prefetch_factor=2 if num_workers > 0 else None,
619	        )
620	        self.val_loader = DataLoader(
621	            self.val_set,
622	            batch_size=self.cfg["data"]["batch_size"],
623	            shuffle=False,
624	            num_workers=num_workers,
625	            collate_fn=collate_fn,
626	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
627	            prefetch_factor=2 if num_workers > 0 else None,
628	        )
629	
630	        print(
631	            f"  [curriculum] Epoch {epoch}: stage '{stage['name']}' "
632	            f"(types={active_types}, gumbel_tau={target_tau}, data_recomputed)"
633	        )
634	
635	    def fit(self):
636	        total_params = sum(p.numel() for p in self.model.parameters())
637	        print(f"IDSP Multi-Type EQ Estimator Training")
638	        print(f"  Device: {self.device}")
639	        print(f"  Parameters: {total_params:,}")
640	        print(
641	            f"  Train batches: {len(self.train_loader)}, Val batches: {len(self.val_loader)}"
642	        )
643	        print(f"  Max epochs: {self.max_epochs}")
644	        print(f"  Receptive field: {self.model.receptive_field_frames} frames")
645	        print()
646	
647	        consecutive_nan_epochs = 0
648	
649	        for epoch in range(1, self.max_epochs + 1):
650	            t0 = time.time()
651	            self._apply_curriculum_stage(epoch)
652	            train_loss = self.train_one_epoch(epoch)
653	
654	            # Check for NaN weights after training
655	            if self._has_nan_weights():
656	                print(
657	                    f"  ERROR: NaN detected in model weights after epoch {epoch}. Stopping."
658	                )
659	                break
660	
661	            val_loss, metrics = self.validate(epoch)
662	            self.scheduler.step()
663	            elapsed = time.time() - t0
664	
665	            # Track consecutive NaN epochs (death spiral detection)
666	            if math.isnan(val_loss):
667	                consecutive_nan_epochs += 1
668	                if consecutive_nan_epochs >= 3:
669	                    print(
670	                        f"  ERROR: val_loss has been NaN for {consecutive_nan_epochs} consecutive epochs. Stopping."
671	                    )
672	                    break
673	            else:
674	                consecutive_nan_epochs = 0
675	
676	            is_best = val_loss < self.best_val_loss
677	            if is_best:
678	                self.best_val_loss = val_loss
679	            self.save_checkpoint(epoch, val_loss, is_best)
680	
681	            self.history.append(
682	                {
683	                    "epoch": epoch,
684	                    "train_loss": train_loss,
685	                    "val_loss": val_loss,
686	                    "metrics": metrics,
687	                    "time_s": elapsed,
688	                }
689	            )
690	
691	            print(
692	                f"Epoch {epoch}/{self.max_epochs} ({elapsed:.1f}s) "
693	                f"train={train_loss:.4f} val={val_loss:.4f}"
694	            )
695	
696	        hist_path = Path("checkpoints") / "training_history.json"
697	        with open(hist_path, "w") as f:
698	            json.dump(self.history, f, indent=2, default=str)
699	        print(f"\nTraining complete. Best val loss: {self.best_val_loss:.4f}")
700	        print(f"History saved to {hist_path}")
701	
702	
703	if __name__ == "__main__":
704	    trainer = Trainer()
705	    trainer.fit()
706

"""
Training script for the multi-type IDSP EQ Estimator.

Uses StreamingTCNModel with MultiTypeEQLoss (Hungarian matching,
permutation-invariant parameter regression, type classification).
On-the-fly synthetic multi-type data generation.

Optimization features (QLoRA/Unsloth/DeepSpeed-inspired):
  - 8-bit AdamW optimizer (bitsandbytes) for reduced memory
  - torch.compile for graph-level optimization
  - Gradient checkpointing for TCN stacks
  - Fused Triton kernels for gated activation
  - DeepSpeed ZeRO-2 integration for multi-GPU
  - Pin memory + async GPU transfer
  - Precomputed dataset caching to disk
"""

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader, random_split
from model_tcn import StreamingTCNModel
from dsp_frontend import STFTFrontend
from loss_multitype import MultiTypeEQLoss, HungarianBandMatcher
from dataset import SyntheticEQDataset, collate_fn
import yaml
from pathlib import Path
import time
import json
import math
import os
from contextlib import nullcontext

try:
    import bitsandbytes as bnb

    HAS_BITSANDBYTES = True
except ImportError:
    HAS_BITSANDBYTES = False

try:
    import deepspeed

    HAS_DEEPSPEED = True
except ImportError:
    HAS_DEEPSPEED = False


def load_config(path="conf/config.yaml"):
    with open(path, "r") as f:
        return yaml.safe_load(f)


class Trainer:
    def __init__(self, config_path="conf/config.yaml"):
        self.cfg = load_config(config_path)
        self.device = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")

        data_cfg = self.cfg["data"]
        model_cfg = self.cfg["model"]
        loss_cfg = self.cfg["loss"]
        trainer_cfg = self.cfg["trainer"]

        self.num_bands = data_cfg["num_bands"]
        self.sample_rate = data_cfg["sample_rate"]
        self.max_epochs = trainer_cfg["max_epochs"]
        self.log_every = trainer_cfg["log_every_n_steps"]
        self.n_fft = data_cfg.get("n_fft", 2048)

        # Optimization flags from config
        self.use_8bit_optimizer = (
            trainer_cfg.get("use_8bit_optimizer", False) and HAS_BITSANDBYTES
        )
        self.use_torch_compile = trainer_cfg.get("use_torch_compile", False)
        self.use_gradient_checkpointing = trainer_cfg.get(
            "use_gradient_checkpointing", False
        )
        self.use_activation_quantization = trainer_cfg.get(
            "use_activation_quantization", False
        )
        self.use_deepspeed = trainer_cfg.get("use_deepspeed", False) and HAS_DEEPSPEED
        self.precompute_cache_path = trainer_cfg.get("precompute_cache_path", None)

        if self.use_8bit_optimizer:
            print("  [opt] Using 8-bit AdamW optimizer (bitsandbytes)")
        if self.use_torch_compile:
            print("  [opt] Using torch.compile for graph optimization")
        if self.use_gradient_checkpointing:
            print("  [opt] Using gradient checkpointing for TCN stacks")
        if self.use_activation_quantization:
            print("  [opt] Using INT8 activation quantization")
        if self.use_deepspeed:
            print("  [opt] Using DeepSpeed ZeRO-2 optimization")

        # Dataset with precomputed mel-spectrograms
        self.train_dataset = SyntheticEQDataset(
            num_bands=self.num_bands,
            sample_rate=self.sample_rate,
            duration=1.5,
            n_fft=self.n_fft,
            size=10000,
            gain_range=tuple(data_cfg["gain_bounds"]),
            freq_range=tuple(data_cfg["freq_bounds"]),
            q_range=tuple(data_cfg["q_bounds"]),
            type_weights=data_cfg.get("type_weights", None),
            precompute_mels=True,
            n_mels=data_cfg.get("n_mels", 128),
        )

        # Try loading precomputed cache from disk
        if self.precompute_cache_path and self.train_dataset.load_precomputed(
            self.precompute_cache_path
        ):
            print(f"  [data] Loaded cached dataset from {self.precompute_cache_path}")
        else:
            # Precompute all samples with mel-spectrograms upfront
            self.train_dataset.precompute()
            # Save cache if path specified
            if self.precompute_cache_path:
                self.train_dataset.save_precomputed(self.precompute_cache_path)

        n_train = int(len(self.train_dataset) * data_cfg["train_split"])
        n_val = int(len(self.train_dataset) * data_cfg["val_split"])
        n_test = len(self.train_dataset) - n_train - n_val
        self.train_set, self.val_set, self.test_set = random_split(
            self.train_dataset,
            [n_train, n_val, n_test],
            generator=torch.Generator().manual_seed(42),
        )

        # Pin memory for async GPU transfer
        pin_memory = self.device.type == "cuda"
        num_workers = data_cfg.get("num_workers", 0)

        self.train_loader = DataLoader(
            self.train_set,
            batch_size=data_cfg["batch_size"],
            shuffle=True,
            num_workers=num_workers,
            collate_fn=collate_fn,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
            drop_last=True,
            prefetch_factor=2 if num_workers > 0 else None,
        )
        self.val_loader = DataLoader(
            self.val_set,
            batch_size=data_cfg["batch_size"],
            shuffle=False,
            num_workers=num_workers,
            collate_fn=collate_fn,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
            prefetch_factor=2 if num_workers > 0 else None,
        )

        # Model
        enc_cfg = model_cfg.get("encoder", {})
        self.model = StreamingTCNModel(
            n_mels=data_cfg.get("n_mels", 128),
            embedding_dim=enc_cfg.get("embedding_dim", 128),
            num_bands=self.num_bands,
            channels=enc_cfg.get("channels", 128),
            num_blocks=enc_cfg.get("num_blocks", 4),
            num_stacks=enc_cfg.get("num_stacks", 2),
            sample_rate=self.sample_rate,
            n_fft=self.n_fft,
        ).to(self.device)

        # STFT frontend
        self.frontend = STFTFrontend(
            n_fft=self.n_fft,
            hop_length=data_cfg.get("hop_length", 256),
            win_length=self.n_fft,
            mel_bins=data_cfg.get("n_mels", 128),
            sample_rate=self.sample_rate,
        ).to(self.device)

        # Loss
        self.criterion = MultiTypeEQLoss(
            n_fft=self.n_fft,
            sample_rate=self.sample_rate,
            lambda_param=loss_cfg.get("lambda_param", 0.0),
            lambda_gain=loss_cfg.get("lambda_gain", 3.0),
            lambda_freq=loss_cfg.get("lambda_freq", 1.0),
            lambda_q=loss_cfg.get("lambda_q", 0.5),
            lambda_type=loss_cfg.get("lambda_type", 0.5),
            lambda_spectral=loss_cfg.get("lambda_spectral", 1.0),
            lambda_hmag=loss_cfg.get("lambda_hmag", 0.3),
            lambda_activity=loss_cfg.get("lambda_activity", 0.1),
            lambda_spread=loss_cfg.get("lambda_spread", 0.05),
            lambda_embed_var=loss_cfg.get("lambda_embed_var", 0.3),
            lambda_contrastive=loss_cfg.get("lambda_contrastive", 0.05),
            embed_var_threshold=loss_cfg.get("embed_var_threshold", 0.1),
        )

        # Hungarian matcher for fair validation metrics (matches target ordering to predictions)
        self.matcher = HungarianBandMatcher(lambda_freq=1.0, lambda_q=0.5)

        # Optimizer: 8-bit or standard
        if self.use_8bit_optimizer:
            self.optimizer = bnb.optim.AdamW8bit(
                self.model.parameters(),
                lr=model_cfg["learning_rate"],
                weight_decay=model_cfg["weight_decay"],
            )
        else:
            self.optimizer = torch.optim.AdamW(
                self.model.parameters(),
                lr=model_cfg["learning_rate"],
                weight_decay=model_cfg["weight_decay"],
            )
        self.scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(
            self.optimizer, T_max=self.max_epochs, eta_min=1e-6
        )

        # LR warmup: linear warmup over first 500 steps, then hand off to CosineAnnealing
        warmup_steps = 500
        warmup_scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.LinearLR(
            self.optimizer, start_factor=1e-3, end_factor=1.0, total_iters=warmup_steps
        )
        self.scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.SequentialLR(
            self.optimizer,
            schedulers=[warmup_scheduler, self.scheduler],
            milestones=[warmup_steps],
        )

        self.gradient_accumulation_steps = trainer_cfg.get(
            "gradient_accumulation_steps", 1
        )
        self.global_step = 0
        self.best_val_loss = float("inf")
        self.history = []

        # bf16-mixed precision: autocast context (no GradScaler needed for bf16)
        self.use_bf16 = trainer_cfg.get("precision", "").lower() == "bf16-mixed"
        if self.use_bf16:
            device_type = "cuda" if self.device.type == "cuda" else "cpu"
            self.autocast_ctx = torch.autocast(device_type, dtype=torch.bfloat16)
        else:
            self.autocast_ctx = nullcontext()

        # Curriculum stages from config
        self.curriculum_stages = self.cfg.get("curriculum", {}).get("stages", [])
        self._current_stage_idx = -1

        # DeepSpeed initialization
        if self.use_deepspeed:
            ds_config_path = trainer_cfg.get(
                "deepspeed_config", "conf/deepspeed_config.json"
            )
            if os.path.exists(ds_config_path):
                with open(ds_config_path, "r") as f:
                    ds_config = json.load(f)
                self.model, self.optimizer, _, _ = deepspeed.initialize(
                    model=self.model,
                    optimizer=self.optimizer,
                    config=ds_config,
                )
                print(f"  [opt] DeepSpeed initialized with config: {ds_config_path}")
            else:
                print(
                    f"  [opt] WARNING: DeepSpeed config not found at {ds_config_path}, disabling"
                )
                self.use_deepspeed = False

        # torch.compile after DeepSpeed init to avoid conflicts
        if self.use_torch_compile:
            self.model = torch.compile(self.model)
            print("  [opt] torch.compile applied to model")

    def _prepare_input(self, batch):
        """Get mel-spectrogram from precomputed cache or compute on-the-fly."""
        if "wet_mel" in batch:
            # Use precomputed mel-spectrogram
            return batch["wet_mel"].to(self.device, non_blocking=True)
        # Fallback: compute from audio
        wet_audio = batch["wet_audio"].to(self.device, non_blocking=True)
        mel_spec = self.frontend.mel_spectrogram(wet_audio)
        return mel_spec.squeeze(1)

    def train_one_epoch(self, epoch):
        self.model.train()
        epoch_loss = 0.0
        n_batches = 0
        n_nan_batches = 0

        # Accumulate per-component losses for epoch-level logging
        component_accum = {}

        self.optimizer.zero_grad(set_to_none=True)

        for batch_idx, batch in enumerate(self.train_loader):
            mel_frames = self._prepare_input(batch)
            target_gain = batch["gain"].to(self.device, non_blocking=True)
            target_freq = batch["freq"].to(self.device, non_blocking=True)
            target_q = batch["q"].to(self.device, non_blocking=True)
            target_ft = batch["filter_type"].to(self.device, non_blocking=True)

            # Forward (wrapped in autocast for bf16-mixed precision)
            with self.autocast_ctx:
                output = self.model(mel_frames)
                pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q = output["params"]

                # Ground truth frequency response
                target_H_mag = self.model.dsp_cascade(
                    target_gain,
                    target_freq,
                    target_q,
                    n_fft=self.n_fft,
                    filter_type=target_ft,
                )

                # Loss (pass embedding for anti-collapse regularization)
                total_loss, components = self.criterion(
                    pred_gain,
                    pred_freq,
                    pred_q,
                    output["type_logits"],
                    output["H_mag"],
                    target_gain,
                    target_freq,
                    target_q,
                    target_ft,
                    target_H_mag,
                    embedding=output["embedding"],
                )

            # Skip batch if loss is NaN (from early training instability)
            if not torch.isfinite(total_loss):
                self.optimizer.zero_grad(set_to_none=True)
                n_nan_batches += 1
                if n_nan_batches >= 10:
                    print(
                        f"  [train] WARNING: {n_nan_batches} NaN batches in epoch {epoch}, stopping epoch early"
                    )
                    break
                continue

            # Scale loss for gradient accumulation
            scaled_loss = total_loss / self.gradient_accumulation_steps

            if self.use_deepspeed:
                self.model.backward(scaled_loss)
            else:
                scaled_loss.backward()

            if (batch_idx + 1) % self.gradient_accumulation_steps == 0:
                if not self.use_deepspeed:
                    grad_norm = torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(
                        self.model.parameters(), max_norm=1.0
                    )
                    if not torch.isfinite(grad_norm):
                        self.optimizer.zero_grad(set_to_none=True)
                        n_nan_batches += 1
                        continue

                    # Per-parameter gradient norm monitoring (logged every N steps)
                    if self.global_step % self.log_every == 0:
                        grad_parts = []
                        for name, param in self.model.named_parameters():
                            if param.grad is not None and param.grad.norm() > 0:
                                if "gain" in name:
                                    grad_parts.append(
                                        ("gain", param.grad.norm().item())
                                    )
                                elif "freq" in name or "log_freq" in name:
                                    grad_parts.append(
                                        ("freq", param.grad.norm().item())
                                    )
                                elif "q_head" in name:
                                    grad_parts.append(("q", param.grad.norm().item()))
                                elif "classif" in name or "type" in name:
                                    grad_parts.append(
                                        ("type", param.grad.norm().item())
                                    )
                        if grad_parts:
                            grouped = {}
                            for k, v in grad_parts:
                                grouped.setdefault(k, []).append(v)
                            avg_grads = {k: sum(v) / len(v) for k, v in grouped.items()}
                            grad_str = " | ".join(
                                f"grad_{k}={v:.4f}"
                                for k, v in sorted(avg_grads.items())
                            )
                            print(f"  [grads] step={self.global_step} {grad_str}")

                    self.optimizer.step()
                else:
                    self.model.step()

                self.optimizer.zero_grad(set_to_none=True)
                self.global_step += 1

            epoch_loss += total_loss.item()
            n_batches += 1

            # Accumulate per-component losses
            for k, v in components.items():
                val = v.item() if isinstance(v, torch.Tensor) else v
                component_accum[k] = component_accum.get(k, 0.0) + val

            if self.global_step % self.log_every == 0:
                lr = self.optimizer.param_groups[0]["lr"]
                parts = [
                    f"step={self.global_step} loss={total_loss.item():.4f} lr={lr:.2e}"
                ]
                for k, v in components.items():
                    parts.append(f"{k}={v:.4f}")
                # Log embedding variance diagnostic (monitors anti-collapse health)
                if "embed_var_loss" in components:
                    embed_var = output["embedding"].var(dim=0).mean().item()
                    parts.append(f"embed_var={embed_var:.4f}")
                print(f"  [train] " + " | ".join(parts))

        if n_nan_batches > 0:
            print(f"  [train] Epoch {epoch}: {n_nan_batches} NaN batches skipped")

        # Per-component epoch averages
        if n_batches > 0:
            comp_strs = []
            for k in sorted(component_accum.keys()):
                avg = component_accum[k] / n_batches
                comp_strs.append(f"{k}={avg:.4f}")
            print(f"  [train] Epoch {epoch} components: " + " | ".join(comp_strs))

        return epoch_loss / max(n_batches, 1)

    @torch.no_grad()
    def validate(self, epoch):
        self.model.eval()
        val_loss = 0.0
        n_batches = 0
        param_maes = {"gain": [], "gain_raw": [], "freq": [], "q": [], "type_acc": []}

        for batch in self.val_loader:
            mel_frames = self._prepare_input(batch)
            target_gain = batch["gain"].to(self.device, non_blocking=True)
            target_freq = batch["freq"].to(self.device, non_blocking=True)
            target_q = batch["q"].to(self.device, non_blocking=True)
            target_ft = batch["filter_type"].to(self.device, non_blocking=True)

            with self.autocast_ctx:
                output = self.model(mel_frames)
                pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q = output["params"]

                target_H_mag = self.model.dsp_cascade(
                    target_gain,
                    target_freq,
                    target_q,
                    n_fft=self.n_fft,
                    filter_type=target_ft,
                )

                total_loss, _ = self.criterion(
                    pred_gain,
                    pred_freq,
                    pred_q,
                    output["type_logits"],
                    output["H_mag"],
                    target_gain,
                    target_freq,
                    target_q,
                    target_ft,
                    target_H_mag,
                    embedding=output["embedding"],
                )

            val_loss += total_loss.item()
            n_batches += 1

            # Hungarian matching for fair MAE computation
            matched_gain, matched_freq, matched_q = self.matcher(
                pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q, target_gain, target_freq, target_q
            )

            # Metrics (matched = fair, raw = for comparison)
            param_maes["gain"].append((pred_gain - matched_gain).abs().mean().item())
            param_maes["gain_raw"].append((pred_gain - target_gain).abs().mean().item())
            param_maes["freq"].append(
                (torch.log2(pred_freq / (matched_freq + 1e-8))).abs().mean().item()
            )
            param_maes["q"].append(
                (torch.log10(pred_q / (matched_q + 1e-8))).abs().mean().item()
            )
            param_maes["type_acc"].append(
                (output["filter_type"] == target_ft).float().mean().item()
            )

        avg_val_loss = val_loss / max(n_batches, 1)
        metrics = {k: sum(v) / len(v) for k, v in param_maes.items() if v}

        print(
            f"  [val] epoch={epoch} val_loss={avg_val_loss:.4f} "
            f"gain_mae={metrics.get('gain', 0):.2f}dB "
            f"gain_raw={metrics.get('gain_raw', 0):.2f}dB "
            f"freq_mae={metrics.get('freq', 0):.3f}oct "
            f"q_mae={metrics.get('q', 0):.3f}dec "
            f"type_acc={metrics.get('type_acc', 0):.1%}"
        )
        return avg_val_loss, metrics

    def save_checkpoint(self, epoch, val_loss, is_best=False):
        ckpt_dir = Path("checkpoints")
        ckpt_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)

        if self.use_deepspeed:
            # DeepSpeed handles checkpointing
            ckpt_path = ckpt_dir / f"epoch_{epoch:03d}"
            self.model.save_checkpoint(str(ckpt_path), tag=f"epoch_{epoch}")
            if is_best:
                self.model.save_checkpoint(str(ckpt_dir / "best"), tag="best")
        else:
            state = {
                "epoch": epoch,
                "model_state_dict": self.model.state_dict(),
                "optimizer_state_dict": self.optimizer.state_dict(),
                "val_loss": val_loss,
                "global_step": self.global_step,
            }
            path = ckpt_dir / f"epoch_{epoch:03d}.pt"
            torch.save(state, path)
            if is_best:
                best_path = ckpt_dir / "best.pt"
                torch.save(state, best_path)
            print(f"  Saved checkpoint: {path}" + (" (best)" if is_best else ""))

    def _has_nan_weights(self):
        """Check if any model parameter contains NaN."""
        for p in self.model.parameters():
            if not torch.isfinite(p).all():
                return True
        return False

    def _apply_curriculum_stage(self, epoch):
        """Determine and apply curriculum stage based on epoch number."""
        if not self.curriculum_stages:
            return

        # Determine stage from cumulative epoch boundaries
        cumulative = 0
        stage_idx = 0
        stage_start_epoch = 0
        for i, stage in enumerate(self.curriculum_stages):
            prev_cumulative = cumulative
            cumulative += stage["epochs"]
            if epoch <= cumulative:
                stage_idx = i
                stage_start_epoch = prev_cumulative + 1
                break
        else:
            stage_idx = len(self.curriculum_stages) - 1

        # Skip if stage hasn't changed (but still do intra-stage annealing)
        stage = self.curriculum_stages[stage_idx]

        # Intra-stage Gumbel temperature annealing
        target_tau = stage.get("gumbel_temperature", 1.0)
        stage_epochs = stage["epochs"]
        epoch_in_stage = epoch - stage_start_epoch
        if stage_idx > 0:
            prev_tau = self.curriculum_stages[stage_idx - 1].get(
                "gumbel_temperature", 1.0
            )
        else:
            prev_tau = 1.0

        # Smooth linear annealing from prev_tau to target_tau within stage
        progress = epoch_in_stage / max(stage_epochs - 1, 1)
        current_tau = prev_tau + (target_tau - prev_tau) * progress
        current_tau = max(current_tau, 0.05)  # floor
        self.model.param_head.gumbel_temperature.fill_(current_tau)

        # Only re-precompute data when stage changes
        if stage_idx == self._current_stage_idx:
            return

        self._current_stage_idx = stage_idx

        # Restrict active filter types for the dataset sampler
        active_types = stage.get("filter_types", None)
        if active_types is not None:
            all_types = ["peaking", "lowshelf", "highshelf", "highpass", "lowpass"]
            weights = [0.0] * len(all_types)
            for t in active_types:
                if t in all_types:
                    weights[all_types.index(t)] = 1.0 / len(active_types)
            self.train_dataset.type_weights = weights

        # Apply param ranges if specified
        param_ranges = stage.get("param_ranges", {})
        if "gain" in param_ranges:
            self.train_dataset.gain_range = tuple(param_ranges["gain"])
        if "q" in param_ranges:
            self.train_dataset.q_range = tuple(param_ranges["q"])

        # Re-precompute dataset with new type_weights/param_ranges.
        # Without this, the cached samples from the previous stage are still used.
        self.train_dataset.precompute()

        # Re-split dataset since underlying data changed
        n_train = int(len(self.train_dataset) * self.cfg["data"]["train_split"])
        n_val = int(len(self.train_dataset) * self.cfg["data"]["val_split"])
        n_test = len(self.train_dataset) - n_train - n_val
        self.train_set, self.val_set, self.test_set = random_split(
            self.train_dataset,
            [n_train, n_val, n_test],
            generator=torch.Generator().manual_seed(42),
        )

        pin_memory = self.device.type == "cuda"
        num_workers = self.cfg["data"].get("num_workers", 0)
        self.train_loader = DataLoader(
            self.train_set,
            batch_size=self.cfg["data"]["batch_size"],
            shuffle=True,
            num_workers=num_workers,
            collate_fn=collate_fn,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
            drop_last=True,
            prefetch_factor=2 if num_workers > 0 else None,
        )
        self.val_loader = DataLoader(
            self.val_set,
            batch_size=self.cfg["data"]["batch_size"],
            shuffle=False,
            num_workers=num_workers,
            collate_fn=collate_fn,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
            prefetch_factor=2 if num_workers > 0 else None,
        )

        print(
            f"  [curriculum] Epoch {epoch}: stage '{stage['name']}' "
            f"(types={active_types}, gumbel_tau={target_tau}, data_recomputed)"
        )

    def fit(self):
        total_params = sum(p.numel() for p in self.model.parameters())
        print(f"IDSP Multi-Type EQ Estimator Training")
        print(f"  Device: {self.device}")
        print(f"  Parameters: {total_params:,}")
        print(
            f"  Train batches: {len(self.train_loader)}, Val batches: {len(self.val_loader)}"
        )
        print(f"  Max epochs: {self.max_epochs}")
        print(f"  Receptive field: {self.model.receptive_field_frames} frames")
        print()

        consecutive_nan_epochs = 0

        for epoch in range(1, self.max_epochs + 1):
            t0 = time.time()
            self._apply_curriculum_stage(epoch)
            train_loss = self.train_one_epoch(epoch)

            # Check for NaN weights after training
            if self._has_nan_weights():
                print(
                    f"  ERROR: NaN detected in model weights after epoch {epoch}. Stopping."
                )
                break

            val_loss, metrics = self.validate(epoch)
            self.scheduler.step()
            elapsed = time.time() - t0

            # Track consecutive NaN epochs (death spiral detection)
            if math.isnan(val_loss):
                consecutive_nan_epochs += 1
                if consecutive_nan_epochs >= 3:
                    print(
                        f"  ERROR: val_loss has been NaN for {consecutive_nan_epochs} consecutive epochs. Stopping."
                    )
                    break
            else:
                consecutive_nan_epochs = 0

            is_best = val_loss < self.best_val_loss
            if is_best:
                self.best_val_loss = val_loss
            self.save_checkpoint(epoch, val_loss, is_best)

            self.history.append(
                {
                    "epoch": epoch,
                    "train_loss": train_loss,
                    "val_loss": val_loss,
                    "metrics": metrics,
                    "time_s": elapsed,
                }
            )

            print(
                f"Epoch {epoch}/{self.max_epochs} ({elapsed:.1f}s) "
                f"train={train_loss:.4f} val={val_loss:.4f}"
            )

        hist_path = Path("checkpoints") / "training_history.json"
        with open(hist_path, "w") as f:
            json.dump(self.history, f, indent=2, default=str)
        print(f"\nTraining complete. Best val loss: {self.best_val_loss:.4f}")
        print(f"History saved to {hist_path}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    trainer = Trainer()
    trainer.fit()

file.content:
# Configuration for the IDSP Multi-Type EQ Blind Estimator

experiment_name: "idsp-eq-multitype-blind-estimator"
seed: 42

data:
  num_bands: 5
  sample_rate: 44100
  audio_duration: 3.0
  n_fft: 2048
  hop_length: 256
  n_mels: 128
  gain_bounds: [-24.0, 24.0]
  freq_bounds: [20.0, 20000.0]
  q_bounds: [0.1, 10.0]
  filter_types: ["peaking", "lowshelf", "highshelf", "highpass", "lowpass"]
  type_weights: [0.5, 0.15, 0.15, 0.1, 0.1]
  train_split: 0.8
  val_split: 0.1
  test_split: 0.1
  batch_size: 32
  num_workers: 4

model:
  encoder:
    type: "tcn"
    n_mels: 128
    embedding_dim: 128
    channels: 128
    num_blocks: 4
    num_stacks: 2
    kernel_size: 3
  num_bands: 5
  learning_rate: 0.0002
  weight_decay: 0.00001

loss:
  # Combined param weight disabled — use independent per-parameter weights
  lambda_param: 0.0
  lambda_gain: 3.0
  lambda_freq: 1.0
  lambda_q: 0.5
  lambda_type: 0.5
  lambda_spectral: 1.0
  lambda_hmag: 0.3
  lambda_activity: 0.1
  lambda_spread: 0.05
  lambda_freq_match: 1.0
  lambda_q_match: 0.5
  # Anti-collapse regularization: reduce after encoder fix
  lambda_embed_var: 0.2
  lambda_contrastive: 0.05
  embed_var_threshold: 0.1

trainer:
  max_epochs: 80
  accelerator: "auto"
  devices: 1
  precision: "bf16-mixed"
  log_every_n_steps: 10
  fast_dev_run: false
  gradient_accumulation_steps: 4
  # Optimization flags (QLoRA/Unsloth/DeepSpeed-inspired)
  use_8bit_optimizer: false
  use_torch_compile: false
  use_gradient_checkpointing: false
  use_activation_quantization: false
  use_deepspeed: false
  deepspeed_config: "conf/deepspeed_config.json"
  precompute_cache_path: null

curriculum:
  stages:
    - name: "peaking_warmup"
      epochs: 10
      filter_types: ["peaking"]
      param_ranges:
        gain: [-12.0, 12.0]
        q: [0.3, 5.0]
      lambda_type: 1.0
      gumbel_temperature: 1.0
    - name: "multitype_fixed"
      epochs: 20
      filter_types: ["peaking", "lowshelf", "highshelf", "highpass", "lowpass"]
      param_ranges:
        gain: [-18.0, 18.0]
        q: [0.2, 8.0]
      lambda_type: 2.0
      gumbel_temperature: 0.5
    - name: "full_difficulty"
      epochs: 30
      filter_types: ["peaking", "lowshelf", "highshelf", "highpass", "lowpass"]
      param_ranges:
        gain: [-24.0, 24.0]
        q: [0.1, 10.0]
      lambda_type: 0.5
      gumbel_temperature: 0.1
    - name: "realworld_finetune"
      epochs: 10
      filter_types: ["peaking", "lowshelf", "highshelf", "highpass", "lowpass"]
      param_ranges:
        gain: [-24.0, 24.0]
        q: [0.1, 10.0]
      lambda_type: 0.5
      gumbel_temperature: 0.1
      learning_rate_scale: 0.1

content.content:
1	"""
2	Multi-type EQ estimation loss functions.
3	
4	Key components:
5	- Hungarian matching for permutation-invariant band assignment (DETR-style)
6	- Combined regression + classification loss
7	- Spectral consistency loss for blind estimation
8	- Auxiliary losses (band activity, frequency spread)
9	"""
10	import math
11	
12	import torch
13	import torch.nn as nn
14	import torch.nn.functional as F
15	from scipy.optimize import linear_sum_assignment
16	import numpy as np
17	
18	from loss import MultiResolutionSTFTLoss
19	
20	
21	class HungarianBandMatcher:
22	    """
23	    Solves the band permutation problem using Hungarian (bipartite) matching.
24	
25	    When predicting N EQ bands, the model's band ordering is arbitrary.
26	    This finds the optimal assignment between predicted and ground-truth bands
27	    to minimize parameter distance, following Carion et al. (DETR, 2020).
28	    """
29	
30	    def __init__(self, lambda_freq=1.0, lambda_q=0.5, lambda_gain=2.0):
31	        self.lambda_freq = lambda_freq
32	        self.lambda_q = lambda_q
33	        self.lambda_gain = lambda_gain
34	
35	    def compute_cost_matrix(self, pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q,
36	                            target_gain, target_freq, target_q):
37	        """
38	        Compute pairwise cost matrix between predicted and target bands.
39	
40	        All inputs: (Batch, Num_Bands)
41	        Returns: cost matrix (Batch, Num_Pred, Num_Target)
42	        """
43	        B, N = pred_gain.shape
44	
45	        # Expand for pairwise computation: (B, N, 1) vs (B, 1, N)
46	        pg = pred_gain.unsqueeze(-1)      # (B, N, 1)
47	        tg = target_gain.unsqueeze(-2)    # (B, 1, N)
48	        pf = pred_freq.unsqueeze(-1)
49	        tf = target_freq.unsqueeze(-2)
50	        pq = pred_q.unsqueeze(-1)
51	        tq = target_q.unsqueeze(-2)
52	
53	        # Gain cost: L1 in dB (weighted to balance against octave-scale freq cost)
54	        cost_gain = self.lambda_gain * (pg - tg).abs()
55	
56	        # Frequency cost: L1 in log-space (octaves)
57	        cost_freq = self.lambda_freq * (torch.log(pf + 1e-8) - torch.log(tf + 1e-8)).abs()
58	
59	        # Q cost: L1 in log-space (decades)
60	        cost_q = self.lambda_q * (torch.log(pq + 1e-8) - torch.log(tq + 1e-8)).abs()
61	
62	        # Filter type cost: if available, add a penalty for type mismatch
63	        cost = cost_gain + cost_freq + cost_q  # (B, N, N)
64	
65	        return cost
66	
67	    def match(self, cost_matrix):
68	        """
69	        Solve the assignment problem for each batch element.
70	
71	        Args:
72	            cost_matrix: (Batch, N_pred, N_target)
73	
74	        Returns:
75	            List of (row_indices, col_indices) tuples per batch element
76	        """
77	        B = cost_matrix.shape[0]
78	        assignments = []
79	        for b in range(B):
80	            cost_np = cost_matrix[b].detach().cpu().numpy()
81	            # Guard against NaN/inf from early training instability
82	            cost_np = np.nan_to_num(cost_np, nan=0.0, posinf=1e6, neginf=-1e6)
83	            row_ind, col_ind = linear_sum_assignment(cost_np)
84	            assignments.append((row_ind, col_ind))
85	        return assignments
86	
87	    def __call__(self, pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q,
88	                 target_gain, target_freq, target_q):
89	        """
90	        Find optimal matching and reorder targets.
91	
92	        Returns:
93	            target_gain_matched, target_freq_matched, target_q_matched
94	            — ground truth reordered to match predicted band ordering
95	        """
96	        cost = self.compute_cost_matrix(
97	            pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q,
98	            target_gain, target_freq, target_q
99	        )
100	        assignments = self.match(cost)
101	
102	        B, N = pred_gain.shape
103	        device = pred_gain.device
104	
105	        matched_gain = torch.zeros_like(target_gain)
106	        matched_freq = torch.zeros_like(target_freq)
107	        matched_q = torch.zeros_like(target_q)
108	
109	        for b in range(B):
110	            row_ind, col_ind = assignments[b]
111	            # col_ind[i] is the ground-truth band index that matches predicted band row_ind[i]
112	            # We want: matched_target[predicted_i] = ground_truth[col for predicted_i]
113	            perm = torch.zeros(N, dtype=torch.long, device=device)
114	            for r, c in zip(row_ind, col_ind):
115	                perm[r] = c
116	            matched_gain[b] = target_gain[b, perm]
117	            matched_freq[b] = target_freq[b, perm]
118	            matched_q[b] = target_q[b, perm]
119	
120	        return matched_gain, matched_freq, matched_q
121	
122	
123	class PermutationInvariantParamLoss(nn.Module):
124	    """
125	    Parameter regression loss with Hungarian matching for band ordering.
126	    Uses Huber loss for robustness to outliers.
127	    """
128	
129	    def __init__(self, lambda_freq=1.0, lambda_q=0.5):
130	        super().__init__()
131	        self.matcher = HungarianBandMatcher(lambda_freq, lambda_q)
132	        self.huber = nn.HuberLoss(delta=5.0)
133	
134	    def forward(self, pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q,
135	                target_gain, target_freq, target_q):
136	        """
137	        All inputs: (Batch, Num_Bands)
138	        Returns: scalar loss
139	        """
140	        matched_gain, matched_freq, matched_q = self.matcher(
141	            pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q,
142	            target_gain, target_freq, target_q
143	        )
144	
145	        loss_gain = self.huber(pred_gain, matched_gain)
146	        loss_freq = self.huber(
147	            torch.log(pred_freq + 1e-8),
148	            torch.log(matched_freq + 1e-8)
149	        )
150	        loss_q = self.huber(
151	            torch.log(pred_q + 1e-8),
152	            torch.log(matched_q + 1e-8)
153	        )
154	
155	        return loss_gain, loss_freq, loss_q
156	
157	
158	class MultiTypeEQLoss(nn.Module):
159	    """
160	    Combined loss for multi-type blind EQ estimation.
161	
162	    Components:
163	    - L_param: Permutation-invariant parameter regression (Huber)
164	    - L_type: Filter type classification (cross-entropy)
165	    - L_spectral: MR-STFT spectral consistency
166	    - L_hmag: Frequency response magnitude L1
167	    - L_activity: Band activity regularization
168	    - L_spread: Frequency spread regularization
169	    - L_embed_var: Embedding variance regularization (anti-collapse)
170	    - L_contrastive: Spectral contrastive loss (anti-collapse)
171	
172	    Anti-collapse mechanism:
173	    The TCN encoder can collapse to producing near-identical embeddings for
174	    all inputs (cosine distance ~0.006). This makes the parameter head's job
175	    impossible — it receives no discriminative information to decode.
176	    L_embed_var directly penalizes low variance across batch dimensions,
177	    forcing the encoder to maintain diverse representations. L_contrastive
178	    goes further by encouraging embeddings from different mel profiles to
179	    occupy distinct regions of the embedding space.
180	    """
181	
182	    def __init__(self, n_fft=2048, sample_rate=44100,
183	                 lambda_param=1.0, lambda_gain=2.0, lambda_freq=1.0, lambda_q=0.5,
184	                 lambda_type=0.5,
185	                 lambda_spectral=1.0, lambda_hmag=0.3,
186	                 lambda_activity=0.1, lambda_spread=0.05,
187	                 lambda_embed_var=0.5, lambda_contrastive=0.1,
188	                 embed_var_threshold=0.1):
189	        super().__init__()
190	        self.lambda_param = lambda_param
191	        self.lambda_gain = lambda_gain
192	        self.lambda_freq = lambda_freq
193	        self.lambda_q = lambda_q
194	        self.lambda_type = lambda_type
195	        self.lambda_spectral = lambda_spectral
196	        self.lambda_hmag = lambda_hmag
197	        self.lambda_activity = lambda_activity
198	        self.lambda_spread = lambda_spread
199	        self.lambda_embed_var = lambda_embed_var
200	        self.lambda_contrastive = lambda_contrastive
201	        self.embed_var_threshold = embed_var_threshold
202	
203	        self.param_loss = PermutationInvariantParamLoss()
204	        self.type_loss = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)
205	        self.mr_stft = MultiResolutionSTFTLoss()
206	        self.huber = nn.HuberLoss(delta=5.0)
207	
208	    def forward(self, pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q,
209	                pred_type_logits, pred_H_mag,
210	                target_gain, target_freq, target_q,
211	                target_filter_type, target_H_mag,
212	                pred_audio=None, target_audio=None,
213	                active_band_mask=None, embedding=None):
214	        """
215	        Compute total loss.
216	
217	        Args:
218	            pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q: (B, N) predicted parameters
219	            pred_type_logits: (B, N, 5) predicted type logits
220	            pred_H_mag: (B, F) predicted frequency response
221	            target_gain, target_freq, target_q: (B, N) ground truth params
222	            target_filter_type: (B, N) ground truth type indices
223	            target_H_mag: (B, F) ground truth frequency response
224	            pred_audio: (B, T) optional reconstructed audio
225	            target_audio: (B, T) optional target audio
226	            active_band_mask: (B, N) optional mask for active bands
227	
228	        Returns:
229	            total_loss: scalar
230	            components: dict of individual loss values
231	        """
232	        components = {}
233	
234	        # --- Anti-collapse losses (computed first so they appear early in logs) ---
235	
236	        # 7. Embedding variance regularization (L_embed_var)
237	        # Prevents encoder collapse by penalizing embeddings that have low variance
238	        # across the batch. When all embeddings are identical (collapse), per-dimension
239	        # variance drops to zero and this loss fires. The threshold allows variance
240	        # above it to pass for free — it only activates when the encoder starts to
241	        # collapse. This is a direct, per-batch diagnostic that requires no auxiliary
242	        # network or negative sampling.
243	        loss_embed_var = torch.tensor(0.0, device=pred_gain.device)
244	        if embedding is not None:
245	            # Variance of each embedding dimension across the batch, then average
246	            embed_var = embedding.var(dim=0).mean()
247	            loss_embed_var = F.relu(self.embed_var_threshold - embed_var)
248	        components["embed_var_loss"] = loss_embed_var
249	
250	        # 8. Spectral contrastive loss (L_contrastive)
251	        # Encourages embeddings from different mel profiles to be dissimilar.
252	        # This complements L_embed_var: variance regularization prevents a trivial
253	        # collapse to a single point, but the encoder could still produce a narrow
254	        # manifold. Contrastive loss explicitly pushes embeddings apart by penalizing
255	        # high pairwise cosine similarity. The formulation is simple: minimize the
256	        # mean pairwise cosine similarity, which ensures the encoder uses the full
257	        # embedding space rather than a thin subspace.
258	        loss_contrastive = torch.tensor(0.0, device=pred_gain.device)
259	        if embedding is not None and embedding.shape[0] > 1:
260	            # Normalize embeddings to unit sphere for cosine similarity
261	            embed_norm = F.normalize(embedding, dim=1)
262	            # Pairwise cosine similarity matrix (B, B)
263	            sim_matrix = torch.mm(embed_norm, embed_norm.t())
264	            # Exclude self-similarity (diagonal is always 1.0)
265	            B = sim_matrix.shape[0]
266	            mask = ~torch.eye(B, dtype=torch.bool, device=sim_matrix.device)
267	            mean_sim = sim_matrix[mask].mean()
268	            # Clamp similarity to prevent log(negative) = NaN and gradient
269	            # explosion when sim approaches 1.0 (gradient ~1/(1-sim) → huge)
270	            mean_sim_clamped = torch.clamp(mean_sim.float(), max=0.95)
271	            loss_contrastive = -torch.log(1.0 - mean_sim_clamped + 1e-3)
272	            loss_contrastive = torch.clamp(loss_contrastive, max=5.0)
273	        components["contrastive_loss"] = loss_contrastive
274	
275	        # 1. Parameter regression (permutation-invariant)
276	        loss_gain, loss_freq, loss_q = self.param_loss(
277	            pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q,
278	            target_gain, target_freq, target_q
279	        )
280	        components["loss_gain"] = loss_gain
281	        components["loss_freq"] = loss_freq
282	        components["loss_q"] = loss_q
283	
284	        # Use independent per-parameter weights when lambda_param is disabled (0.0)
285	        if self.lambda_param > 0:
286	            loss_param = self.lambda_param * (loss_freq + loss_q) + self.lambda_gain * loss_gain
287	        else:
288	            loss_param = self.lambda_gain * loss_gain + self.lambda_freq * loss_freq + self.lambda_q * loss_q
289	        components["param_loss"] = loss_param
290	
291	        # 2. Filter type classification
292	        # Reshape for cross-entropy: (B*N, 5) and (B*N,)
293	        B, N, C = pred_type_logits.shape
294	        loss_type = self.type_loss(
295	            pred_type_logits.reshape(B * N, C),
296	            target_filter_type.reshape(B * N)
297	        )
298	        components["type_loss"] = loss_type
299	
300	        # 3. Frequency response magnitude L1
301	        loss_hmag = F.l1_loss(
302	            torch.log(pred_H_mag + 1e-8),
303	            torch.log(target_H_mag + 1e-8)
304	        )
305	        components["hmag_loss"] = loss_hmag
306	
307	        # 4. Spectral consistency (if audio provided)
308	        if pred_audio is not None and target_audio is not None:
309	            sc_loss, log_loss = self.mr_stft(pred_audio, target_audio)
310	            loss_spectral = sc_loss + log_loss
311	            components["spectral_loss"] = loss_spectral
312	        else:
313	            loss_spectral = torch.tensor(0.0, device=pred_gain.device)
314	            components["spectral_loss"] = loss_spectral
315	
316	        # 5. Band activity regularization
317	        if active_band_mask is not None:
318	            inactive_mask = ~active_band_mask
319	            loss_activity = (pred_gain * inactive_mask.float()).abs().mean()
320	        else:
321	            loss_activity = torch.tensor(0.0, device=pred_gain.device)
322	        components["activity_loss"] = loss_activity
323	
324	        # 6. Frequency spread regularization (bounded to prevent pushing frequencies to extremes)
325	        if pred_freq.shape[1] > 1:
326	            log_freq = torch.log(pred_freq + 1e-8)
327	            # Pairwise log-frequency differences
328	            diff = log_freq.unsqueeze(-1) - log_freq.unsqueeze(-2)  # (B, N, N)
329	            spread = diff.abs().mean()
330	            # The maximum achievable mean pairwise spread is bounded by the
331	            # log-frequency range (~6.9 octaves for 20-20000 Hz).  Clamp so
332	            # the repulsive force saturates instead of growing without bound.
333	            max_spread = math.log(20000.0 / 20.0)  # ~6.9
334	            spread = torch.clamp(spread, max=max_spread)
335	            loss_spread = -spread  # Maximize spread
336	        else:
337	            loss_spread = torch.tensor(0.0, device=pred_gain.device)
338	        components["spread_loss"] = loss_spread
339	
340	        # Total: independent per-parameter weights (no combined lambda_param wrapper)
341	        total_loss = (
342	            self.lambda_gain * loss_gain
343	            + self.lambda_freq * loss_freq
344	            + self.lambda_q * loss_q
345	            + self.lambda_type * loss_type
346	            + self.lambda_spectral * loss_spectral
347	            + self.lambda_hmag * loss_hmag
348	            + self.lambda_activity * loss_activity
349	            + self.lambda_spread * loss_spread
350	            + self.lambda_embed_var * loss_embed_var
351	            + self.lambda_contrastive * loss_contrastive
352	        )
353	
354	        return total_loss, components
